description: Phase 2 controlled expansion — adds Ligue 1 and Eredivisie hubs with 10 curated clubs total.

leagues.name: Ligue 1
leagues.description: France's top division mixes Paris Saint-Germain's modern star power with historic clubs from Marseille, Lyon, Monaco, Lille, and other strong regional football cities.
leagues.styleOfPlay: Athletic, fast, and development-heavy. Ligue 1 often gives young players real minutes, while top clubs blend direct transitions with technical attacking midfielders and quick wide players.
famousClubs: Paris Saint-Germain — Paris superclub and modern title force
Marseille — southern giant with European pedigree
Monaco — talent factory from the principality
Lille — northern club with recent title success
leagues.historySummary: French football has produced waves of elite talent, from Marseille's European triumph to Lyon's domestic dynasty and PSG's Qatar-backed era. The league is often watched for the next breakout star as much as for the title race.
rivalries: Choc des Olympiques — Lyon vs Marseille
Derby du Nord — Lille vs Lens
leagues.fanGuide: Start with Le Classique to understand the emotional map: Paris against Marseille. Then follow Lyon and Monaco for academy and talent-development storylines.

leagues.name: Eredivisie
leagues.description: The Netherlands' top league is a classic football learning lab: Ajax, PSV, and Feyenoord drive the title story, while smaller clubs often develop future European stars.
leagues.styleOfPlay: Attack-minded and academy-driven, with a strong emphasis on technical midfielders, wingers, possession ideas, and flexible Dutch positional concepts.
famousClubs: Ajax — Amsterdam academy and European history
PSV Eindhoven — title regular with attacking identity
Feyenoord — Rotterdam power and De Kuip atmosphere
AZ Alkmaar — smart recruitment and youth development
FC Twente — strong regional support in Enschede
famousPlayers: Johan Cruyff — Ajax legend and Dutch football icon
Ruud Gullit — Feyenoord and PSV graduate before Milan fame
Ruud van Nistelrooy — PSV striker before Manchester United
leagues.historySummary: Dutch clubs shaped modern football through Total Football, academy coaching, and European success. Ajax, PSV, and Feyenoord remain the big three, but the league's broader value is how quickly young players can become important.
rivalries: PSV vs Ajax — title-race heavyweight matchup
Twente vs Heracles — eastern Netherlands derby
leagues.fanGuide: Learn the big three first, especially De Klassieker. Watch how Dutch teams build attacks and trust young players; it is one of the best leagues for learning football patterns.

stub.shortHistory: PSG grew from a young Paris club into France's dominant modern force, with the Qatari ownership era turning the Parc des Princes into a global football stage.
stub.fanGuide: Dark blue, red, and Paris identity. Le Classique against Marseille is the fixture to learn first; European expectations shape every season.
legends: Zlatan Ibrahimovic — striker who defined early QSI dominance

stub.shortHistory: Marseille are France's southern giant and the first French club to win the European Cup. Their support, noise, and suspicion of Paris drive much of Ligue 1's emotion.
stub.fanGuide: White and sky blue; the Velodrome is the sound. Le Classique vs PSG is about geography, culture, and power as much as football.
legends: Didier Drogba — explosive striker and cult hero
Basila Boli — 1993 European Cup final scorer

stub.shortHistory: Lyon dominated France in the 2000s and built one of Europe's most respected academy pipelines, producing technical midfielders and forwards for bigger stages.
stub.fanGuide: White kits with red and blue; academy pride matters. The derby against Saint-Etienne is the local grudge, while Marseille games carry national weight.

stub.shortHistory: Monaco play in the French system from the principality and are known for waves of elite talent, from European finals to the Mbappe-led title team of 2017.
stub.fanGuide: Red and white diagonal shirts; smaller stadium, big talent reputation. Watch Monaco to spot future stars before they move.

stub.shortHistory: Lille are a northern club with a modern recruitment reputation and a famous 2021 title that interrupted PSG's dominance.
stub.fanGuide: Red, navy, and northern pride. The Derby du Nord against Lens is the fixture that explains local identity.

stub.shortHistory: Ajax are the academy and ideas club: Total Football, European Cups, and a conveyor belt of players who shape bigger leagues.
stub.fanGuide: White shirt with a red central stripe; Amsterdam confidence. De Klassieker against Feyenoord is essential.
legends: Johan Cruyff — club and football philosophy icon

stub.shortHistory: PSV began as the Philips works team and became one of Dutch football's title powers, known for attacking teams and export-ready forwards.
stub.fanGuide: Red and white stripes; Eindhoven identity. PSV vs Ajax often feels like a title checkpoint.
legends: Arjen Robben — winger before European fame

stub.shortHistory: Feyenoord are Rotterdam's working-class giant, with European history and one of the Netherlands' most intense stadium atmospheres at De Kuip.
stub.fanGuide: Red and white halves; Rotterdam edge. De Klassieker vs Ajax is the first rivalry to learn.

stub.shortHistory: AZ are a smart-recruitment club with title history, strong analytics reputation, and a productive youth pathway outside the Dutch big three.
stub.fanGuide: Red and white from Alkmaar; watch for clever recruitment and academy players taking senior responsibility.

stub.shortHistory: Twente carry strong regional support in Enschede and won the Dutch title in 2010, a rare break in the big-three pattern.
stub.fanGuide: Red shirts; De Grolsch Veste is loud when Twente are pushing near the top. The Heracles derby is the local one.
